rulings.Case Title: KEVIN WILLIAMS VS. ERIC SAFIRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Eric Safire And John Scott'S Petition To Confirm Justice William Stein'S Appointment As Arbitrator
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 28, 2012, LINE 18, Defendants Eric Safire And John Scott'S Petition To Confirm Justice William Stein'S Appointment As Arbitrator. Hearing required because petitioner has already stated that he contests the tentative ruling, which is: Granted. Since Ruth Glick is unable to serve as the arbitrator, arbitrate this matter, Justice William Stein (retired) is appointed the arbitrator per the court order issued March 22, 2012. Mr. Williams' objections are not well-founded as he has already used his one peremptory challenge and has not shown good cause to disqualify Justice Stein. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 28, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF FRANK ALIOTO'S Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents Set 2 And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 28, 2012, LINE 20, Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents Set 2 And Sanctions. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Ernst Halperin as follows: Granted with respect to Request for Production 15 and 16, denied with respect to the remaining Requests for Production, and sanctions are denied. State Fund shall produce an electronic copy of the monthly emails sent from State Fund to Ingenix. The court adds that further responses and production of documents shall be served within 20 days. A further hearing is set for April 17, 2012 at 9:30 am in Department 302 to determine whether the public interest and any ongoing investigation by State Fund and/or the Alameda County District Attorney's Office will not be jeopardized by compelling responses to Requests for production 8, 10, 14 & 17. Within two days, parties shall serve notice of the hearing on the Alameda County District Attorney's office so that the District Attorney may have the opportunity to submit briefing to the court on that question and appear at the hearing. The court adds that all papers be filed and served per CCP § 1005(b) and all courtesy copies be provided to Department 302 per Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 28, 2012, LINE 20, Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogs No. 1. The court adds that further responses shall be served within 20 days. A further hearing is set for April 17, 2012 at 9:30 am in Department 302 to determine whether the public interest and any ongoing investigation by State Fund and/or the Alameda County District Attorney's Office will not be jeopardized by compelling responses to Interrogatories 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 19, 20, 21, 40, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47, and 48. Within two days, parties shall serve notice of the hearing on the Alameda County District Attorney's office so that the District Attorney may have the opportunity to submit briefing to the court on that question and appear at the hearing. The court adds that all papers be filed and served per CCP § 1005(b) and all courtesy copies be provided to Department 302 per Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Amended Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Qualified
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 28, 2012, LINE 20, Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Amended Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Qualified. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Ernst Halperin as follows: Granted. The deposition shall go forward at a time mutually convenient to the parties, under the following conditions: Defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund shall designate and produce at the deposition those of its officers, directors, managing agents, employees, or agents who are most qualified to testify on its behalf as to topics 2, 3, and 4 in Plaintiff's notice of deposition dated November 8, 2011, to the extent of any information known or reasonably available to the State Compensation Insurance Fund. Those topics are: 2. The basis for State Compensation Insurance Fund's contention that Dr. Cabayan fraudulently billed State Fund by upcoding, unbudling and/or in the provision of interpreter services. 3. Interviews and/or statements obtained by State Fund and-or its agents in any form from Cabayan patients, Cabayan current or former employees and/or referring doctors/chiropractors which form the basis or support the contention of fraudulent billing against Vatche Cabayan, M.D. and/or Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation. 4. On CPT coding, unbudling and the Official Medical Fee Schedule as it pertains to Workers Compensation patients to which State Fund adheres and/or relies. State Fund's production of such person(s) most knowledgeable is without prejudice to State Fund's ability to assert any privilege, including but not limited to the fraud investigation privilege under Insurance Code Section 1877.4, as a basis for refusing to answer a question. State Fund shall produce at the deposition documents responsive to document categories 3, 4, and 5 in Plaintiff's Notice of the Deposition of State Compensation Insurance Fund's Person(s) Most Knowledgeable dated November 8, 2011. These categories are: (3) Any and all documents including electronic records which refer and/or relate to state Fund's allegations of fraudulent billing by Vatche Cabayan, M.D. and/or Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation. (4) Any and all documents including electronic records upon which State Fund basis its contention that Dr. Cabayan fraudulently billed State Fund by upcoding and/or unbundling services. (5) Any and all documents including electronic records which support State Fund's allegation that interpreter services were fraudulently billed by Vatche Cabayan, M.D., and/or Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation. The above topics are listed without prejudice to State Fund's ability to assert any privilege, including but not limited to the fraud investigation privilege under Insurance Code Section 1877.4, as a basis for refusing to produce a document or group of documents. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs Under Ccp 425.16(C) And For Sanctions Under Ccp 128.5
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 28, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANTS LISA LEEBOVE and STEVE STEINBERG'S Motion For An Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs Under CCP 425.16(C) And For Sanctions Under CCP 128.5 is Granted. Attorney fees of $7,325 and costs of $589.60 are awarded to Ms. Leebove and Mr. Steinberg. The anti-SLAPP motion to the Second Cross-Complaint was frivolous, as indicated by the Court of Appeal's opinion. The anti-SLAPP motion to the Cross-Complaint was not frivolous as shown by Ms. Leebove and Mr. Steinberg's dismissal of the Cross-Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DEAN KARDASSAKIS VS. JIMMY KARD AKA JIMMY G. KARDASSAKIS, INDIVIDUALLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To To Written Disco And Req For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 28, 2012, Line 9. PLAINTIFF DEAN KARDASSAKIS' Motion To Compel Responses To To Written Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA SCHOOL BOARDS ASSOCIATION et al VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Memo Of P And A In Support Of Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 28, 2012, LINE 10, PETITIONER CALIFORNIA SCHOOL BOARDS ASSOCIATION, ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA SCHOOL ADMINISTRATORS, SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, LOS ANGELES UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT and TURLOCK UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate is Denied. Nothing in the language of Proposition 98 or its ballot materials precludes the Legislature from assigning revenue to a special fund that previously had been deposited in the general fund. Nor, if the Legislature does so, there is nothing in the language of Proposition 98 or its ballot materials which requires the Legislature to "rebench" that revenue for purposes of Test 1. The hearing will be at 11:30am, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
